features.summary: Tesla reported record Q1 2022 results despite significant challenges including COVID-related Shanghai factory shutdowns and supply chain disruptions. The company achieved record deliveries, revenue, and operating margins of 19%, with GAAP automotive gross margin reaching 32.9%. Production was impacted by a month-long Shanghai shutdown, but the facility has resumed operations. New factories in Berlin and Austin began production in late March and early April respectively, with initial ramp expected to be slow but accelerating exponentially.
features.management_outlook: Tesla maintains confidence in achieving 50% annual growth in vehicle production for 2022, with potential for 60% growth. The company expects to produce over 1.5 million vehicles in 2022, with Q2 production similar to Q1 due to Shanghai disruptions, but Q3 and Q4 substantially higher. Management announced plans for a dedicated robotaxi vehicle optimized for autonomy (no steering wheel or pedals) targeting volume production in 2024, and confirmed Cybertruck volume production for next year. The company aims to reach 20 million units annually long-term.
features.risks: Tesla faces significant inflationary pressures with suppliers requesting 20-30% cost increases, particularly in raw materials like lithium where spot prices are 10x extraction costs. The company has substantial exposure to raw material costs (10-15% of cost structure) with various contract lag effects. COVID-related shutdowns remain a risk, though current indications suggest Shanghai operations are recovering. New factory ramp inefficiencies in Austin and Berlin will pressure Q2 margins, and 4680 battery production must reach volume by early 2023 to avoid becoming a constraint for 2023 vehicle targets.
features.key_drivers: Higher vehicle pricing continues to benefit financials as the company works through a substantial backlog with wait times extending into next year. The transition to structural battery packs and front/rear castings is reducing manufacturing complexity and costs, with body shop size dropping over 60%. Tesla is rapidly scaling 4680 battery production and expects to achieve volume production by Q3-Q4 2022. The company is expanding its insurance business to 80% of US customers by year-end and is seeing strong adoption with improved safety outcomes through real-time driving feedback.
